_readme: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the model landscape. Consumed by (a) the paper 'Using AI Safely and Ethically in Research', Section 2, and (b) the llm-guide web tool, Model landscape section. Update here only, then run shared/sync-models.sh to push into the app. This is the fix for the two artefacts drifting apart.
verification_note: Entries with "verify": true were drawn from secondary reporting and MUST be checked against the primary source before publication or before minting a DOI. aaIndex = Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index (0-100 composite). The index was revised to v4.1 during 2026, so scores are NOT comparable across index versions. null means we have no sourced figure - do not substitute a guess.
benchmark_policy: The previous version of the web tool displayed hardcoded MMLU / HumanEval / MATH figures. Those are not reproduced here because no verified current figures were available for the 2026 model set. Showing a single sourced composite with an explicit as-of date is more defensible than three unsourced numbers.
caveats: Composite 'intelligence' scores aggregate very different underlying tasks - always cite the methodology alongside a score.
Rankings changed materially within a single month during drafting. Every number here is perishable.
Context windows and parameter counts are vendor-stated where given; several vendors do not disclose.
'Open weight' is not 'open source': weights and a licence are published, but often not training data or code. Licences vary per model and must be checked individually.

models.name: Claude Fable 5
models.strengths: Top of the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index at time of writing; reasoning and coding

models.name: GPT-5.6
models.strengths: Broad general capability; most widely deployed, via ChatGPT

models.name: Kimi K3
models.strengths: Among the top few models of any kind; agentic coding
models.note: Released 16 July 2026; weights reportedly not published until 27 July 2026 - confirm before describing as open-weight.

models.name: GPT-5.5
models.strengths: Still widely deployed; strong default for writing and knowledge work

models.name: GLM-5.2
models.strengths: Reported strongest open-weight model overall; reasoning and code

models.name: DeepSeek V4 Pro
models.strengths: MoE efficiency, strong reasoning and code

models.name: Gemini 3.x Pro
models.ctx: very large (1M+ on recent Gemini releases)
models.strengths: Long context, multimodal, deep Workspace integration
models.note: Exact current point release not confirmed.

models.name: Qwen3.7 Max
models.strengths: Multilingual, strong general capability; permissive licence on prior Qwen3 releases

models.name: Llama 4
models.note: Llama Community Licence is not OSI-approved.

models.name: Gemma 3
models.strengths: Runs on modest hardware - a practical option for keeping sensitive data local

models.name: Phi-4-mini
models.strengths: Laptop-capable, strong for its size, STEM-oriented

models.name: Claude Haiku 4.5
models.strengths: Fastest Claude tier; low latency, cost-efficient

embedding_models._note: Compare on the public MTEB leaderboard rather than vendor claims; match the model to the task (retrieval vs clustering vs classification).

structural_points: The gap between the best open-weight and best proprietary models has narrowed to a few points on composite benchmarks.
Chinese labs (Moonshot, DeepSeek, Z.ai/Zhipu, Alibaba, MiniMax) hold most top open-weight positions - relevant to procurement, provenance and data-governance decisions.
Local open-weight models are the practical route to using AI on confidential or personal data without it leaving your institution.
